description:
You are the site administrator and have been handed a plain-text user database located at  

/home/user/user_accounts.txt  

Each line is in the following fixed format (single space between the two fields, no trailing spaces):  

USERNAME STATUS  

Example (already present in the file):  
john ENABLED  

Your goal: create a new report file that lists only the disabled accounts.

Requirements for the report:
1. Create the file /home/user/disabled_accounts.log (overwrite if it already exists).
2. Read /home/user/user_accounts.txt, select every line whose STATUS field is the exact word “DISABLED”.
3. Extract only the USERNAME from those lines (drop the STATUS field).
4. Sort the resulting USERNAMEs in strictly ascending (A–Z) order.
5. For every USERNAME in the sorted list, write a line to /home/user/disabled_accounts.log in this exact format (no additional spaces or characters):  
DISABLED: USERNAME  
6. The file must end with a single trailing newline (i.e., the last line is terminated with “\n”).

The entire job must be achieved with one single compound terminal command (you may use pipes and redirections, but launch only once from the prompt).

Example of the exact expected contents of /home/user/disabled_accounts.log after you finish (for the current dataset in /home/user/user_accounts.txt):

DISABLED: david
DISABLED: mary
DISABLED: susan

(No blank lines before, between or after, and each line ends with “\n”.)

When you are done, you should be able to display the file with

cat /home/user/disabled_accounts.log

and see exactly the three lines shown above.
truth:
Initial files present before the task starts:

1. /home/user/user_accounts.txt  
Contents (each line terminated by “\n”):  
john ENABLED  
mary DISABLED  
peter ENABLED  
susan DISABLED  
david DISABLED  

(The order is exactly as above.)

No other relevant files exist.

Expected result after the agent completes the task:

1. /home/user/disabled_accounts.log  
Exact contents (each line terminated by “\n”, no extra whitespace):  
DISABLED: david  
DISABLED: mary  
DISABLED: susan  

File permissions: default user-writable regular file.

Automated tests will:
• Verify that /home/user/disabled_accounts.log exists.  
• Verify that it contains exactly the three lines above, in that order, each ending with “\n”, and nothing else.  
• Ensure the original /home/user/user_accounts.txt is unchanged.  
• Confirm the agent used only a single shell command (history ‑c is disabled, so test will count the number of executed commands against one).
